content.text: State each of the following theorems and prove one of them.

problem.text: Schoenflies Theorem for \(\mathbb{R}^2\). Does the corresponding statement hold for \(\mathbb{R}^3\)? Explain.

content.text: For the following problems give proofs as well as conclusions. Your proofs should be clear and detailed, you should show all your work, and your writing must be legible.

problem.text: Compute the fundamental group of the projective plane, \(P^2\).
Compute the homology groups and the Euler characteristic of the Klein bottle.
Let \(D^n=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n:|x|\leq 1\}\) be the \(n\)-dimensional disk. Show that every continuous map \(f:S^3\to D^n\) is homotopic to a constant map.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that the Cantor set \(C\) is totally disconnected.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(f:C\to[0,1]\) is a continuous map from the Cantor set to the closed unit interval, must \(f\) omit a value in \([0,1]\)? (I.e., must there exist a \(y\in[0,1]\) such that \(f(x)=y\) has no solution \(x\in C\)?)

problem.text: For \(i=1,2,3,\ldots\), let \(X_i\) be a great circle in the \(2\)-sphere \(S^2=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^3:|x|=1\}\). Show that \(S^2\neq\bigcup_{i=1}^{\infty}X_i\).
